rag_rules: Latched expressions must appear only in component implementations, not in type contracts
Binary expressions in guarantees must have consistent boolean types on both sides
Invalid type references in AGREE expressions should be traced to root causes and fixed by resolving identifiers or removing clauses
generic_rules: AGREE contracts must be placed in appropriate scopes: type annexes for assumptions/guarantees, implementation annexes for bindings
Guarantee expressions must use only visible identifiers and valid boolean types
When AGREE reports type errors, trace the first unresolved identifier and fix the root cause
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: Use only visible AADL features or eq helpers declared in the same annex for guarantee expressions
AGREE scalar types are int, real, and bool; avoid package-qualified or invalid type names
Latched expressions are valid only for input data ports or event expressions over input event data ports
anti_patterns: Do not place latched expressions in component type contracts; they are only valid in implementations
Do not mix invalid or unresolved identifiers in binary boolean expressions without fixing the root cause
